summary: Demand evidence agents completed and PRD readiness passed.

demand_status.summary: Demand is not PRD-ready; missing slots, blockers, assumptions, or evidence remain.

triage.explanation: Careful route required by hard triggers; factual or risky claims need evidence before PRD.

problem: Existing YOLO demand evidence dispatch API/schema behavior must verify harness boundary audit for full-tool Claude agents before PRD readiness.
status_quo: YOLO demand evidence dispatch exists in a dirty working tree and must be stress-tested with real Claude -p agents.
desired_outcome: Explorer, cross-checker, and verifier cite project paths and boundary audit keeps PRD readiness blocked on missing evidence or target-project mutation.
scope_out: Do not intentionally modify target project files during evidence gathering.
constraints: Full tools may be available; harness boundary audit must detect any target-project mutation.
acceptance_criteria: Explorer, cross-checker, and verifier cite project paths and boundary audit keeps PRD readiness blocked on missing evidence or target-project mutation.
risks: If full-tool agents mutate project files without blocker, demand communication can corrupt target projects.

blockers.message: explorer must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
cross-checker must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
verifier must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
explorer must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
cross-checker must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
verifier must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.

protocol.objective: Find primary project facts for the requested demand without editing files.
protocol.prompt: Read only the relevant files, configs, logs, docs, and current implementation. Return claims with exact paths/lines or source summaries. Separate assumptions from facts.

evidence_tasks.reason: Factual project claims need primary evidence.

protocol.objective: Independently verify high-risk factual claims and challenge the explorer result.
protocol.prompt: Use independent reads or sources. Confirm, weaken, or contradict each claim. Any conflict must become a blocker instead of being averaged away.
protocol.required_when: evidence_policy === cross_check or claim risk is high

protocol.objective: Check whether gathered evidence is enough to support PRD readiness and acceptance criteria.
protocol.prompt: Verify that no assumption is presented as fact, evidence paths/sources are usable, and unresolved missing data is reflected as blockers or assumptions.

evidence_tasks.reason: Verify blockers and assumptions are not promoted to facts.

state.next_action: Run explorer + cross-checker + verifier before treating project claims as facts.

protocol.objective: Check whether gathered evidence is enough to support PRD readiness and acceptance criteria.

agent_results.claim: YOLO demand evidence dispatch implements boundary audit via file snapshot comparison before/after agent execution, with enforcement blocking PRD readiness when mutations occur outside allowed artifact roots.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot computes SHA-256 digests for all project files under project root, excluding allowed write roots (output artifacts), creating a pre-execution baseline.
evidence.why: This function enables detection of any file mutations by comparison with post-execution state.

evidence.summary: diffBoundarySnapshots compares before/after snapshots, returning list of added/modified/deleted files with change type.
evidence.why: This is the core boundary audit mechanism that detects unauthorized mutations.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot called BEFORE agent execution loop to capture baseline state.
evidence.why: Establishes project state before any agents can potentially mutate files.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot called AFTER all agents complete, and diffBoundarySnapshots computes changes stored in result.boundary.
evidence.why: Post-execution audit detects any mutations that occurred during agent runs.

evidence.summary: Any boundary changes become blockers with code BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION, blocking PRD readiness.
evidence.why: Enforces boundary by converting detected mutations into PRD readiness blockers.

evidence.summary: execution_policy sets project_mutation forbidden and declares allowed_write_roots (typically .yolo/demand/evidence/dispatch-*).
evidence.why: Policy declares which write roots are exempt from boundary audit checks.

evidence.summary: Agent hard rules explicitly forbid modifying target project files; must report verification gaps in missing array.
evidence.why: Documents the non-editing constraint enforced on all evidence agents.

evidence.summary: Every evidence record must declare scope (project/external/user/unknown).
evidence.why: Scope declaration enables project-fact validation requiring project-scoped evidence.

evidence.summary: projectEvidenceBlockingIssues verifies project-scoped evidence includes repo-relative paths; returns blocker if missing.
evidence.why: Enforces that existing-project facts must cite concrete project locators.

evidence.summary: Evidence agent results pass through evidenceResultBlockingIssues and projectEvidenceBlockingIssues validation; blocking issues become readiness blockers.
evidence.why: Agent results are validated for both completion status and project evidence requirements.

assumptions: File digest computation works correctly for project files under git and non-git projects.
Agent providers (Claude -p, Codex, custom) do not bypass filesystem and write directly to project roots without going through detected files.
The boundary snapshot excludes output artifact roots correctly, so legitimate evidence writes do not trigger false positives.
risks: If boundary snapshot misses files (e.g., very large files, ignored paths, symlinks), mutations could occur undetected.
If agent provider has direct filesystem access that bypasses file walking (e.g., mounted volumes, special filesystems), mutations could occur outside snapshot detection.
If allowed_write_roots configuration is mis-specified, legitimate writes could block PRD readiness with false-positive mutations.

result.notes: Explorer confirms boundary audit implementation exists via snapshot comparison with enforcement blocking PRD readiness. Cross-check should verify snapshot completeness and agent provider isolation assumptions.

agent_results.claim: YOLO demand evidence dispatch implements complete boundary audit via SHA-256 file snapshot comparison before/after agent execution, with enforcement that converts any detected mutations into PRD readiness blockers via BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION code.

evidence.summary: fileDigest computes SHA-256 hash using createHash('sha256').update(readFileSync(path)).digest('hex'), confirming SHA-256 algorithm.
evidence.why: Confirms the exact hash algorithm used for snapshot integrity verification.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot resolves allowedRoots, uses gitFiles or walkFiles for file discovery, skips allowed roots via isWithin check, computes SHA-256 digests, returns Map.
evidence.why: Independently confirms explorer's claim about snapshot creation and allowed root exclusion logic.

evidence.summary: diffBoundarySnapshots iterates before/after Maps, detects deleted, modified, and added files by comparing presence and digest equality, returns sorted changes array.
evidence.why: Verifies the core change detection logic that identifies mutations.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot(plan.project_root, [plan.output_dir]) executes BEFORE mkdirSync and agent loop, establishing baseline state.
evidence.why: Confirms pre-execution snapshot timing matches explorer's evidence.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot called AFTER agent loop completes at line 640, diffBoundarySnapshots computes changes stored in result.boundary with project_mutation and changes array.
evidence.why: Confirms post-execution audit and boundary result structure.

evidence.summary: boundaryBlockers maps changes to BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers; finalReadiness spreads readiness.blockers with runtimeBlockers and sets prd_ready false when blockers exist.
evidence.why: Verifies the enforcement mechanism that converts mutations into PRD readiness blockers.

evidence.summary: execution_policy sets project_mutation forbidden and allowed_write_roots [repoRelative(outputDir)], declaring boundary constraints.
evidence.why: Confirms policy configuration matches explorer's evidence.

evidence.summary: Agent prompt hard rules: 'do not modify target project files' and 'If verification would require mutation, report it in missing', confirming non-editing constraint.
evidence.why: Verifies agent protocol constraints on evidence gathering.

evidence.summary: Agent prompt requires 'Every evidence record must include scope: project, external, user, or unknown.' and 'Project facts require project-scoped evidence with repo-relative path.'
evidence.why: Confirms scope declaration and path requirements for project evidence.

evidence.summary: projectEvidenceBlockingIssues returns PROJECT_EVIDENCE_PATH_REQUIRED blocker when has_project_scope is true without path locator, or PROJECT_FACT_REQUIRES_PROJECT_EVIDENCE when no project scope.
evidence.why: Independently verifies router validation logic for project evidence requirements.

evidence.summary: Agent results loop calls evidenceResultBlockingIssues, evidenceScopeDeclarationIssues, and projectEvidenceBlockingIssues; blocking issues pushed to blockerList.
evidence.why: Confirms agent result validation integration into readiness blockers.

evidence.summary: gitFiles uses git ls-files -co --exclude-standard -z; walkFiles recursively reads directories excluding .git, node_modules, dist, coverage, .next, .cache.
evidence.why: Verifies file discovery methods and exclusion patterns for snapshot completeness.

evidence.summary: isWithin computes relative(path, root) and checks for absence of .. prefix and non-absolute path, confirming subtree containment logic.
evidence.why: Verifies allowed root exclusion logic prevents false positives from legitimate writes.

evidence.summary: finalReadiness conditional spreads readiness with [...readiness.blockers, ...runtimeBlockers] and prd_ready false when runtimeBlockers exist, confirming blocker enforcement.
evidence.why: Verifies that boundary mutations actually block PRD readiness.

assumptions: git ls-files -co --exclude-standard returns all tracked and untracked files excluding .gitignore patterns.
Agent provider filesystem operations are observable by buildBoundarySnapshot via standard file walking.
SHA-256 digests computed via readFileSync capture complete file content including any agent writes.
Agent provider cannot bypass snapshot detection by writing outside project root or using special filesystem features.
File system state remains stable between snapshot computation and agent execution for non-mutated files.
risks: If git ls-files misses untracked files not covered by walkFiles fallback, mutations could occur undetected.
If agent provider has direct block-device or volume-level filesystem access, snapshot could miss mutations.
If allowedRoots configuration includes project root subdirectories incorrectly, legitimate project mutations could be allowed.
If buildBoundarySnapshot excludes very large files or symlinks via fileDigest null return, those mutations would not be detected.

result.notes: Cross-check confirms all explorer claims via independent code verification. Boundary audit implementation exists with SHA-256 snapshot comparison, before/after timing, and BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blocker enforcement. Router validation integration confirmed. Assumptions align with filesystem and git behavior limitations.

agent_results.claim: Explorer and cross-checker evidence is sufficient and proper: project facts are backed by project-scoped evidence with exact paths and line numbers; assumptions are clearly separated from facts; no missing data remains unreflected; evidence sources are usable and verifiable.

evidence.summary: Both explorer and cross-checker cite identical code ranges with matching line numbers for boundary audit implementation: fileDigest (SHA-256), buildBoundarySnapshot, diffBoundarySnapshots, before/after timing, and BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blocker enforcement.
evidence.why: Independent verification confirms explorer claims are reproducible and specific to exact code locations.

evidence.summary: Both agents cite execution_policy configuration and agent prompt hard rules defining project_mutation forbidden and scope declaration requirements.
evidence.why: Verifies policy constraints and agent protocols are documented in code.

evidence.summary: Both agents cite router validation logic that enforces project evidence requirements and converts blocking issues into readiness blockers.
evidence.why: Confirms integration between evidence dispatch and router validation.

evidence.summary: Cross-checker independently verified file discovery methods (gitFiles, walkFiles) and isWithin containment logic cited by explorer.
evidence.why: Verifies snapshot completeness and allowed root exclusion mechanisms.

evidence.summary: Explorer and cross-checker both return empty missing arrays, no blockers, and pass verdicts, with all evidence having scope=project and source=project_code.
evidence.why: Confirms no unresolved gaps remain and all claims are properly scoped.

evidence.summary: Both agents explicitly list 3-5 assumptions each under assumptions array, separate from evidence array, including filesystem behavior and git limitations.
evidence.why: Confirms assumptions are not promoted to factual claims.

result.notes: Verification confirms explorer and cross-checker evidence is complete and proper. All project facts cite exact repo-relative paths and line numbers. Assumptions are clearly separated and not treated as facts. No missing data remains unreflected. Evidence sources are usable, specific, and independently verified. PRD readiness evidence requirements are satisfied.
